extraction.one_line_summary: DENNIS Rohan led BMC to TTT victory in Nîmes, gaining critical seconds over Sky (3rd) and major gaps on GC favorites ARU and CONTADOR (+30s).
tactical_patterns: BMC measured time on fifth rider, forcing Sky to actively brake before finish line despite superior front group speed.
ATAPUMA crashed first bend; technical urban TTT courses expose handling weaknesses more than pure power deficits early season.
CONTADOR's Trek team lost 30+ seconds despite home-hero status; aging GC squads lack TTT depth even with motivated leaders.
Quick-Step set early 60:04 benchmark that held against 15 teams; small rosters without pure TT specialists struggle to break established times.

rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: Track specialist background translated to driving BMC's technical urban TTT win, suggesting TTT-to-prologue form correlation.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: Sky placed third but had to brake for fifth rider rule, indicating thinner TTT roster depth than 2016 edition.
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: negative: Astana lost 30+ seconds in opening TTT, revealing team weakness that compounds his known late-race vulnerability.
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: negative: Trek squad 30+ seconds down signals retirement-year roster inadequacy around him despite Nîmes homecoming motivation boost.
rider_signals.ATAPUMA Darwin: negative: First-bend crash on technical opening reveals bike-handling liability that will haunt punchy mountain finishes under pressure.
rider_signals.CHAVES Esteban: positive: Orica-Scott 17 seconds back at rank 5 shows strong TTT depth around twin GC options with Yates brothers.
rider_signals.BUCHMANN Emanuel: neutral: Bora team set early reference time; his Tour top-15 form carried over but team lacks stage-winning TTT firepower.
